description: Universal geometric coupling system. Energy-geometric duality: systems optimize through phi-ratio alignment. Mode switching between EXPLORE (innovation) and EXPAND (maintenance). Same physics as seed growth.

geometric_coupling_system.description: Universal base class for any system that couples through geometry. Tracks energy budget, mode state, and field coupling efficiency.

parameters.E: Total energy budget — same as seed energy
parameters.geometry: Current geometric state — how close to φ-alignment
parameters.mode: EXPLORE or EXPAND — same as seed growth modes

field_coupling_efficiency.description: Peaks when geometry matches phi-ratios. Systems naturally evolve toward φ because it's the most efficient coupling geometry.
field_coupling_efficiency.formula: efficiency = f(geometry, φ) — maximized at φ-alignment
field_coupling_efficiency.rosetta_link: This IS why shapes matter. Platonic solids are geometrically optimal. φ appears in all five. The shapes aren't arbitrary — they're the solutions to 'what geometry couples most efficiently?'

get_branching_threshold.description: Determines when the system switches from EXPAND to EXPLORE. Same as seed physics E_branch = k × C(S).
get_branching_threshold.rosetta_link: BRANCHING_K in explore.py. The point where consolidation becomes exploration.

update_energy.description: Track energy consumption. Exploration costs more than expansion. Coupling efficiency reduces cost.
update_energy.rosetta_link: Maintenance cost in sim.py: complexity * 0.1 per tick

mode_switching.description: Systems alternate between exploration (high-energy pattern generation) and expansion (low-energy crystallization). This is universal — it appears in seed physics, in the Rosetta sim, and in every natural system Shadow-Hunting analyzes.

EXPLORE.description: High energy. Innovation. Pattern generation. Search for better geometry.
EXPLORE.efficiency: Low initially, potentially high if new coupling found
EXPLORE.rosetta_mode: explore in seed physics / discover() in exploration engine

EXPAND.description: Low energy. Maintenance. Crystallization. Lock in current geometry.
EXPAND.efficiency: High for current structure, no innovation
EXPAND.rosetta_mode: expand in seed physics / home_base() resting

mode_switching.switching_trigger: Energy relative to branching threshold. Same in all systems. Same equation.

efficiency_reframe.description: The central insight: what standard science calls 'waste' is unmeasured field coupling doing useful work.

examples.standard_model: 75% of energy is 'overhead' — maintenance, not computation
examples.shadow_model: 75% is EM field coupling, glial computation, quantum coherence — all doing work the electrodes can't measure
examples.rosetta_entity: Relevant to any ANIMAL.* entity with FAMILY.F06 (Cognition)

examples.shadow_model: 82-100% efficiency when accounting for multi-scale FRET coupling organized by φ
examples.rosetta_entity: Relevant to PLANT.* entities with FAMILY.F04 (Life)

examples.shadow_model: Rain bands spaced at φ-ratios, spiral coherence predicts intensification
examples.rosetta_entity: Relevant to FIELD.* entities with FAMILY.F17 (Turbulence)

examples.shadow_model: Bioelectric field coupling at φ-coherence drives pattern formation. Voltage gradients are geometric, not just chemical.
examples.rosetta_entity: Relevant to entities with FAMILY.F04 (Life) and FAMILY.F10 (Particle/EM)

efficiency_reframe.rosetta_link: The shadow IS the unexplored merge. The missing variable. The family affinity nobody checked. discover() at depth > 1 finds the shadows in the Rosetta graph.
